articles.title: What to know about the highly mutated new COVID strain found in Mass. wastewater
articles.description:
Nicknamed the “cicada” variant due to its lengthy hibernation, BA.3.2 derives from a strain that first emerged in late 2021 to 2022.

The post What to know about the highly mutated new COVID strain found in Mass. wastewater appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Massachusetts wastewater reveals a new COVID variant, BA.3.2, nicknamed the 'cicada' variant.
BA.3.2 is derived from a strain that emerged between late 2021 and 2022.
Residents should remain vigilant as this variant may affect local health guidelines.

articles.title: DCF workers who authorized removal of kids in Waltham could stand trial, judge rules
articles.description:
A couple is suing the DCF workers who ordered the seizure of their children. This was done without their consent and despite an investigation finding no evidence of abuse, they say.

The post DCF workers who authorized removal of kids in Waltham could stand trial, judge rules appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Couple in Waltham sues DCF workers for unauthorized child removal.
Judge rules DCF workers may face trial for actions taken in 2026.
Potential trial could impact DCF policies and child welfare practices in Boston.

articles.title: 3 New England restaurants among the most anticipated openings, according to Food & Wine
articles.description:
The New England restaurants are included in a list of 12 eateries opening this spring across the country.

The post 3 New England restaurants among the most anticipated openings, according to Food & Wine appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Food & Wine lists three New England restaurants opening this spring.
Twelve eateries nationwide are anticipated, highlighting local culinary growth.
Boston residents can expect new dining options enhancing the local food scene.

articles.title: The Boston Public Library is the star of Kate Quinn’s latest NYT bestseller
articles.description:
The BU alum and former Winthrop resident’s love for Boston sparked “The Astral Library.”

The post The Boston Public Library is the star of Kate Quinn’s latest NYT bestseller appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Kate Quinn's novel 'The Astral Library' highlights the Boston Public Library's significance.
Quinn, a BU alum and former Winthrop resident, published the book in March 2026.
The book's popularity boosts local interest in Boston's cultural institutions.
